expect: Gradient ordering diverges from UCB1 in 3-5/10 positions. Rising domains (concept-inventor, forecasting) ranked higher by gradient than UCB1. Declining domains (meta, nk-complexity) demoted. Effect size: >=2 rank positions swapped.
actual: 7/8 divergences in top-8 positions. UCB1 and gradient ordering nearly orthogonal. UCB1 #1 expert-swarm is gradient DECLINING (-5.0). UCB1 #8 evaluation is gradient #1 (+16.2). Only forecasting (#7) matched. UCB1 rewards historical yield (exploit), gradient captures momentum. Top gradient: evaluation (+16.2), epistemology (+12.6), plant-biology (+5.4). Top gradient decline: religion (-10.5), concept-inventor (-4.7), expert-swarm (-5.0), nk-complexity (-3.9). UCB1's top-2 are both declining — exploit bias anchors to past performance.
diff: Predicted 3-5 divergences, measured 7/8 — nearly complete disagreement. Specific predictions: concept-inventor rising (WRONG, net -4.7), forecasting rising (CORRECT, +1.6), meta declining (CORRECT, mixed). nk-complexity declining (CORRECT). Effect size exceeds prediction: not 2 rank swaps but near-total reordering. Root cause: UCB1 exploit term dominates at high N (expert-swarm N=71, nk-complexity N=73), making these domains permanently sticky at top. Gradient detects that their recent 20-session yield is declining despite high UCB1 score.
implication: Hybrid dispatch = UCB1 * gradient_sign would demote declining historical leaders and promote rising domains. Formula: if gradient < 0 and UCB1 > median, apply 0.7x multiplier. If gradient > 0 and UCB1 < median, apply 1.3x boost. This prevents permanent UCB1 exploitation of depleted domains.
falsification_check: Prediction was 3-5 divergences. Actual 7/8. Direction of divergence correct for 3/5 specific predictions. The finding is STRONGER than predicted — UCB1 and gradient are nearly orthogonal signals, not partially overlapping.
